question.text: What is the Short-Term Capital Gains (STCG) tax rate on equity mutual fund units sold within 12 months of purchase (FY 2025-26)?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** From FY 2025-26, equity mutual fund units sold within 12 months attract STCG tax at a flat rate of 20%. This was revised upward from the earlier 15% rate. The holding period for equity funds to qualify as long-term is more than 12 months. **The Trap:** Many people still remember the old 15% rate. The budget revised this to 20%. Do not confuse this with the 12.5% LTCG rate which applies after 12 months. **Cross-Exam Context:** Also tested in V-A and Series XXIV with more complex scenarios. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always remind retired investors that selling equity funds within a year costs them 20% tax on gains. Encourage holding beyond 12 months to benefit from the lower 12.5% LTCG rate with a Rs. 1.25 lakh annual exemption.
